name: Nzambi
description: Nzambi is the supreme creator god in Bakongo mythology, the traditional belief system of the Kongo people of Central Africa. Nzambi is often considered a remote and transcendent deity, responsible for the initial creation of the universe and all that exists within it. While Nzambi created the world, he is not directly involved in the daily affairs of humans. He is often seen as the ultimate source of life, power, and destiny. Some interpretations portray Nzambi as a monotheistic god, while others acknowledge the existence of lesser spirits and deities that serve as intermediaries between Nzambi and humanity. His power is absolute, encompassing all aspects of existence. He is the ultimate judge and the source of all moral authority. Many Bakongo rituals and prayers are ultimately directed towards Nzambi, even if they are mediated through other spirits or ancestors. Though distant, Nzambi represents the ultimate good and the final destination of the soul after death. He is the embodiment of cosmic order and the foundation of all being. He is also associated with rain and fertility, ensuring the continuation of life. Some stories depict Nzambi as having created the first man and woman and providing them with the knowledge and resources to thrive in the world. He is considered to be both benevolent and just, rewarding those who live according to his laws and punishing those who transgress. His name is invoked in times of crisis, seeking his intervention and guidance.
subtitle: The Supreme Creator God

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Nzambi created the universe from nothing, shaping the land, sea, and sky. He then populated it with plants, animals, and eventually, humans.

keyMyths.title: Nzambi and the First Humans
keyMyths.description: Nzambi created the first man and woman and gave them the knowledge to cultivate the land and live in harmony. He also warned them against disobedience, which led to suffering.

keyMyths.title: Nzambi's Distance
keyMyths.description: After creating the world, Nzambi withdrew from direct involvement, leaving it to the care of lesser spirits and ancestors. Humans must strive to live according to his will to find favor.
keyMyths.source: Wyatt MacGaffey, 'Religion and Society in Central Africa'

sources.author: Jan Vansina
